explicit_text: My family and I moved to the United States on September 7th, 2019, after my grandparents who live there guaranteed our schooling and better education. Arriving in the United States was challenging at first because we didn't know English well, but over time, my parents found jobs and my brothers and I made friends through activities like the Vietnamese Eucharistic Youth Movement. I have traveled within the United States, visiting places like California, Wisconsin Dells, and Indianapolis, which helped me feel more connected and hopeful about my future here. Living in the United States feels like a second chance for me to study hard and succeed.

explicit_text: I grew up in Ho Chi Minh City, which is the largest city in Vietnam with over 12 districts. My family lived in Thu Duc district, one of those 12 districts, where my parents ran a tofu and soy milk business based on a secret ancestral recipe. Ho Chi Minh City was my childhood home, where I made many memories with my friends and neighbors before moving to the United States in 2019. Leaving Ho Chi Minh City was hard because I missed my friends and family there deeply.
